node.content:
Who wouldn’t want to win a new 2021 truck of their choice? Exactly!

 

 

For over 8 years now, the San Antonio Youth Educational Support foundation (SA YES) has held an annual summertime truck raffle in partnership with the Ancira Auto Group. All to benefit elementary school children in need of assistance. Ancira provides the ‘truck of the winner’s choice’ from one of their five area dealerships including Chevrolet, Ford, GMC, Nissan or Dodge Ram. Last year’s event raised over $300,000 to benefit SA YES.

 

 

“We provide each child with all of the school supplies they need to be successful in school. Paper, pens, pencils, notebooks, folders, rulers, erasers, glue and Kleenex are given to kids free of charge,” stated Danielle Gunter, Executive Director of SA YES.

 

 

“Drive Away for Education has been a very successful fundraiser for SA YES. Our partnership with Ancira has been incredibly beneficial to our efforts to help area school children succeed in school,” stated Bob Wills who is both the CEO and Founder of The PM Group and the current Board President of SA YES.

 

 

Wills started SA YES 23 years ago along with the Richard Breakie family. One hundred percent of the monies raised for SA YES is done through local fundraising events. The organization gets no support from local, state or federal government agencies. This makes SA YES totally unique in its mission to help area school children in-need.

 

 

Starting in August, SA YES will begin distributing school supplies to area Title 1 designated elementary schools in San Antonio, Corpus Christi and Austin that qualify for support.

 

 

“The mission of SA YES combined with the generous support of key sponsors like Thomas J Henry make our efforts possible to give school kids the tools they need to succeed,” stated PM Group President Fran Yanity.

 

 

The winner of this year’s Drive Away for Education Raffle will be announced on August 11, 2021. Raffle tickets can be purchased by Texas residents only through August 8, 2021 at www.SAYES.org.

 

 

 

 

About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. For over 33 years, the agency has continued to grow and is San Antonio’s largest advertising agency. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including home services, automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profits, real estate and fitness. For two consecutive years now, the Agency has set annual revenue records with the addition of six new clients and through the continued success of our existing clients. Additionally, the agency hosts their annual Charity Gala that to date has raised millions of dollars for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! In 2020 alone, the Agency donated over $1,050,000 to local non-profits. It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
Sunday, July 11th, in the San Antonio Express-News we were able to feature the organization, SJRC, in our latest edition of Making a Difference in San Antonio. SJRC has spent over 37 years caring for families and children most affected by trauma, abuse, and neglect. Although originally tied to a Nevada child welfare organization, for the last five years SJRC has been focused on caring for Texas kids. It was important for their team to serve the local community as they are based out of Bulverde, Texas and know just how many children and families need support.

 

 

The organization offers foster care and adoption services for local foster homes as well as residential foster care. They also provide resources and housing for pregnant and parenting teens, a prevention program, and resources and housing for survivors of child sex trafficking. Being a safe haven for 250 kids every day takes resources and time and as they continue to grow in the number of individuals they can help, they continue to call on the support of the community.

 

 

You can read the full article on MYSA and visit their website to learn more and donate

node.content:
On a recent quarterly earnings call, Costco CFO Richard Galanti stated that “inflation pressures abound.” The selling price for everyday staples like paper goods, meat, cheese, and trash bags are all up at the cash register. Galanti further added that “items shipped across the ocean” have seen “suppliers paying up to double for containers and shipping.”

 

 

A post-pandemic wardrobe refresh of your closet could also become more expensive; Galanti said that certain apparel items are up 3% to 10%. Computer chips, oils, and chemical supplies could see spikes, thanks to product shortages. Paper products could cut into budgets, as rising pulp prices send prices surging 4% to 8%. Meat is increasingly expensive, rising 7% year over year. Meat lovers could get hit with a 20% surge for certain beef products in the last month, according to Galanti.

 

 

So, what is driving up consumer prices so rapidly?

Our analysis leads us to point to multiple, obvious culprits.

  • Excessive federal government spending always leads to inflation according to most economists. Printing cash always has its consequences.
  • New presidential / administration regulations. For example, gasoline prices are up one dollar per gallon with the shutdown of the Keystone pipeline by the Biden administration. This forces America to be more reliant on foreign oil.
  • The threat of rising taxes and fees at the federal, state, and local levels.
  • China! For example, China bought up 5.44 million tons of the world’s rubber supplies in 2020. This has led to massive shortages of tires and other rubber-based products in the United States. American automobile manufacturers are trying to cope with the major supply chain disruptions and its painful for consumers who ultimately bear the cost.
  • Labor force shortages are driving up wages which in-turn leads to inflation of virtually everything.
  • Pent up consumer demand as consumers wait for items to return to sale after being put on long wait lists or backorders.
  • Logistics / supply chain bottlenecks as factories, distributors, etc begin to open back up. Companies are facing increased shipping times and higher shipping costs.

 

 

The PM Group has clients in many retail categories, and they report significant cost increases in many areas of their business. Restaurants chains like IHOP and SILO Elevated Cuisine have multiple financial pain points with rising labor shortages and rising food costs. Beyer Plumbing reports major supply chain cost increases for pipe and water heaters. Sadly, the list is long for most businesses.

node.content:
When first responders lose their life in the line of duty, The 100 Club of San Antonio provides funds to their family for immediate and long-term needs. That’s why The PM Group shows our support to those first responders who dedicate their careers (and their lives) to keep us safe and protected from harm’s way.

 

 

Since 1973, when Law Enforcement Officers and Firefighters pay the ultimate sacrifice with their life, The 100 Club of San Antonio is there. Funds are available to the family for immediate needs, placing a strong emphasis on higher education funding for surviving dependent children.

 

 

The 100 Club is citizen-based and not affiliated with any police, fire or governmental agency. They serve all Local, State and Federal Law-Enforcement and Fire Departments in Bexar County and its municipalities. If any law-enforcement officer, agent or firefighter is based in Bexar County, The 100 Club of San Antonio will be there to support them and their family.

 

 

Their primary goal is to deliver funds to the family within 24 to 48 hours of their loss in order to help with any immediate expenses that will most undoubtedly arise upon the unexpected death of their loved one. Afterward, they continue to support the family and place any dependent children into The 100 Club Survivor Fund, where there are currently 12 Survivor Children receiving five year monthly annuity payments to assist with their education, and an additional 18 minor children in line to receive their assistance.

 

 

That’s why all of our affiliated companies proudly support The 100 Club. The PM Group’s President and Chief Operating Officer Fran Yanity recently presented the organization with a check for $10,000 to help them in their mission to assist the families in our community who have lost a loved one.

 

 

“The PM Group has a long history of supporting The 100 Club and the brave men and woman who make our community a safer place to live. We are honored to do our part in helping them fulfill their mission,” stated Bob Wills, The PM Group’s CEO and Founder.  “When you become the largest advertising and marketing firm in San Antonio, you have an obligation to give back to the community that has done so much for you,” stated Yanity.

node.content:
Together with our ongoing partnership with the San Antonio Express-News, The PM Group featured nonprofit SA YES in Sunday’s Making a Difference in San Antonio feature in SA INC.

 

 

SA YES is an organization that has a long history with The PM Group. For over 23 years they have been supporting thousands of elementary school children with the school supplies necessary to succeed each school year, as well as, grants given to area schools and organizations for programs that promote successful learning. The PM Group has been helping raise funds and our employees generously volunteer their time to help SA YES fundraising events like their annual Taste of Education Dinner and Golf Classic.

 

 

In the recent article, you can learn all about SA YES’ efforts in San Antonio and other South Texas communities each year with their Back-to-Basics program and other educational donations and programs the organization supports. One of those programs is their new ‘Little Locker Program’ that allows students to come and borrow books from the lockers located at Edgewood ISD Family Service Center, Sosa Parent Center, Hutchins Elementary School, Harlandale Middle School, and Terrell Wells Middle School. These lockers also house craft supplies in the summer while school is out in an effort to keep kids active and focused on positive ways to spend their time. SA YES also recently distributed grants to Pleasanton ISD and PAM Media Outreach as part of their YES Grant program which you can read all about in one of our latest blogs.

 

 

None of this would be possible without SA YES’ annual fundraising opportunities, one of which you can get involved with now. Earlier this month, the organization launched their Drive Away for Education Truck Raffle. This campaign allows individuals to buy raffle tickets to win their dream truck valued at $40,000 or other incredible cash prizes like a $4,000 shopping spree or $2,500 in HEB gift cards. Now through August 8th raffle tickets can be purchased as well as the opportunity to be involved in their online auction. There’s also a chance to win several HEB gift cards during weekly prize drawings on WOAI-TV. The grand prize, second, and third place prizes will be drawn on August 11th on WOAI-TV’s 10pm newscast.

 

 

 

 

About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. For over 33 years, the agency has continued to grow and is San Antonio’s largest advertising agency. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including home services, automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profits, real estate and fitness. For two consecutive years now, the Agency has set annual revenue records with the addition of six new clients and through the continued success of our existing clients. Additionally, the agency hosts their annual Charity Gala that to date has raised millions of dollars for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! In 2020 alone, the Agency donated over $1,050,000 to local non-profits. It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
As the pandemic comes to a close, America is reopening its restaurants, businesses, theme parks, concerts and special events…and one of the biggest entertainment events in the country will take place right here in San Antonio: Celebrity Fan Fest!

 

 

Celebrity Fan Fest is a pop culture event held annually by our sister company, PMX Events, and this Texas comic con has scored a major victory by securing Star Wars royalty!  Ewan McGregor, also known as ‘Obi-Wan Kenobi’ is making his first appearance at any pop culture fan festival ever! On top of that talent line-up victory, the 2021 Celebrity Fan Fest has also secured Hayden Christensen who played Anakin Skywalker/Darth Vader in the Star Wars prequels. Both actors are appearing at Celebrity Fan Fest during its 10-day run Friday July 30th through Sunday August 8th. They will be appearing the first weekend only. Dates and event information can be seen at www.CelebrityFanFest.com. The event will take place at Six Flags Fiesta Texas since the Freeman Expo Halls were not available this year.

 

 

Now PMX Events Founder Bob Wills has all the comic cons in America talking about the incredible line-up they have already secured for this year’s show. In addition to McGregor and Christensen, he has secured the stars of the new CW Network hit series ‘Superman & Lois’, several Marvel stars, and other DC stars too! If early sales are any indicator, this show will be a major success.

 

 

“We have fans coming to San Antonio from over 30 U.S. states already, from international locales as well. The data from our ecommerce site reveals the popularity of Celebrity Fan Fest nationally,” Bob Wills stated. “We have never seen such demand in our brief 4-year history. We may have started something wonderful for the San Antonio tourism industry,” Wills added. Celebrity Fan Fest was cancelled in 2020 due to the pandemic like every other show in America.

 

 

PMX Events is a sister company of The PM Group. Celebrity Fan Fest was the first event PMX ever held, and it continues to grow. Last fall, the company held the ‘Freaky Car Show’ at Six Flags Fiesta Texas over four weekends. In the spring, PMX Events created a museum exhibit for the San Antonio Museum of Art called ‘Movie Metal’.  The attraction set all-time, best-ever attendance records for the museum.

 

 

The PM Group’s owners have a massive collection of Hollywood movie and television cars like one of the original ’66 Batmobiles. “Our collection of Hollywood assets is valued in the millions of dollars and the vehicles are in big demand! Our Cars as Stars division has 38 vehicles in its collection that appear all over the country as well as locally,” stated The PM Group’s President and Chief Operating Officer, Fran Yanity.

In 2020 alone, the Agency donated over $1,050,000 to local non-profits. It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
On June 3rd, SA YES presented a STEM grant to Pleasanton ISD in the amount of $6,000. The grant is part of a $15,000 allocation of funds by SA YES to STEM needs at Title 1 schools around the San Antonio area as part of their YES Grant program.

 

 

The funds will be used for students in grades 9 through 12 for an introduction to engineering curriculum and supplies. The goal is to create a solid engineering program of study and then focus on adding other STEM programs. With the funding, PISD will be able to purchase a curriculum developed by engineers which helps the staff provide the industry-specific knowledge and skills to the students.

 

 

Three PM Group employees were on hand for the check presentation. Peggy Wills, Kathy Schmitt and Fran Yanity also serve on the SA YES Board of Directors.

 

 

This latest donation follows a busy season of fundraising for SA YES as well as other YES Grant donations. Earlier in the month, SA YES presented P.A.M. Media Outreach with $4,000 for computers to be used in their program helping inner city youth learn leadership skills.

 

 

 

 

“I am honored to be able to provide Pleasanton ISD and PAM Media Outreach much-needed funds to further their programs and goals to help students,” stated Danielle Gunter, Executive Director of SA YES. “STEM is the future and all students should have the resources they need to make strides in this area,” continued Gunter.

On Tuesday, June 1, San Antonio Sports announced its new Fiesta-sanctioned event, Fiesta FitFest, April 8-10, 2022. The new 3-day event will feature ‘Pedals, Medals and Fun’ for the whole family. From Alpha Warrior events to a 5k/10k run, music and food to the final Tour De France-style cycling event, L’Etape San Antonio, there will be something for everyone to enjoy.

 

 

“This new event puts San Antonio on the international map for competition-based cycling events. We’re excited to help our client, San Antonio Sports, launch this incredible event,’ stated Bob Wills, CEO of The PM Group.

 

 

“San Antonio Sports is pleased to bring fitness talent to Fiesta next year. People of all fitness levels can participate,” said Russ Bookbinder, President and CEO of San Antonio Sports “I’m particularly excited to host L’Etape San Antonio. We believe this event will invigorate the local cycling community, just as the Rock’n’Roll Marathon changed our running scene.”

 

 

The PM Group will be on the launch committee tasked with building the event from the ground level while driving interest in the weekend festivities via marketing and advertising support. Sister agency, Noisy Trumpet, designed the colorful logo for the event and has secured social media influencers to share information about the upcoming event.

 

 

“San Antonio is a thriving, engaging city with so much to offer,” continued Fran Yanity, President/COO of The PM Group, President/CEO of Noisy Trumpet Digital & PR, “The addition of L’Etape San Antonio, the only U.S. stop for Tour de France in 2022, makes our city an easy destination of choice for local, regional, national and international tourism.”

node.content:
The PM Group today operates as San Antonio’s largest full service advertising agency, committed to our clients future growth and success. The PM Group realized many years ago that agency services could not be offered to clients for the sole purpose of driving incremental agency revenue and profits. Instead, we added key in-house services to simplify our clients work life and schedule. We determined that our clients wanted high-level professional marketing services that delivered what they needed — without them having to deal with 2-4 other external vendors – and at a lower cost threshold. Even as the pandemic has been the elephant in the (virtual meeting) room since early 2020, it is not the only hurdle today’s advertising and marketing agencies must overcome for continued growth and success in the future. Average ‘client lifespan’ is one of the biggest challenges ad agencies face today. The two key elements of ‘Value & Time’ were very important to the client lifespan equation. Simplifying a client’s life and delivering the desired marketing results ‘for less’ creates a dynamic agency-client partnership built to last for years.

 

 

Case in point, The PM Group’s average client lifespan exceeds 10+ years. The national ad agency-client lifespan averages only 4.5 years. We have represented IHOP restaurants regionally for 21 years now, personal injury attorney Thomas J. Henry for 14 years and counting and we represented Pizza Hut and Taco Bell DMA co-ops for 27 years before all advertising was taken nationally five years ago.

 

 

Building the proverbial ‘better mousetrap’ centered around the key marketing needs of our clients…and potential clients. However, we had to be certain we provided those services in a ‘best in class’ delivery. We identified those advertising services and started building our full service advertising agency platform with sister companies that could deliver the needed full service marketing agency support as follows:

 

 

Quarter Moon Productions

  • We opened QMP in 2010 as a full-service studio primarily focused on producing television and radio commercials with a staff of five people. Today,18 studio staffers not only produce commercials, but also major television content that airs all across Texas in 17 DMA’s. ‘YOLOTX’ is the most popular QMP-produced TV show that will begin its 5th year in production this coming September.

 

 

Nnovative Marketing Solutions  

  • We opened NMS in 2011 with its primary product ‘The BOOK of FREE’ as a consumer savings book that provided consumers with over $6,000 in annual savings on dining, entertainment, retail and more. Since our inception, BOF has been sold to over 100,000 customers, making other ‘savings books’ irrelevant with many going out of business in the San Antonio/South Texas area. Participation in the BOOK of FREE is FREE to our agency clients.

 

 

Noisy Trumpet Digital & Public Relations   

  • In 2017, Noisy Trumpet Digital & Public Relations opened its doors and has risen to become the second largest public relations firm in the San Antonio market today. The initial staff of five has risen to 14 people today dedicated to delivering clients’ needs swiftly and efficiently. Whether it’s a press conference, building a client website, executing social media campaigns or digital brand awareness, Noisy Trumpet has been delivering nationally-recognized results since its inception.  The PM Group, QMP and Noisy Trumpet often pitch new clients together as a collective group to demonstrate the power of unified collaboration to garner greater results.

 

 

PMX Events

  • November 2018 was the date of the very first event that PMX Events produced. ‘Celebrity Fan Fest’ was an in-house created pop culture comic con held at the JW Marriott Resort in San Antonio. Headliners included Ben Affleck and Jason Momoa and Ray Fisher at this 2-day fan event that attracted over 8,600 attendees. Since then, PMX Events has produced three additional comic cons, including the Freaky Car Show at Six Flags Fiesta Texas during their Hallowfest event in October 2020. In addition, we have a mega World Championship Boxing watch party scheduled for June 26th for our client Thomas J Henry. PMX has demonstrated its ability to put on world-class events. For example, the Celebrity Fan Fest event in 2019 attracted over 36,000 attendees coming from 45 states and 12 counties. The event generated over 1.7 billion impressions for the event and our sponsors.

 

 

CSAV

  • Convention Services Audio-Video was launched in 2020 as an audio video services provider for event organizers. Of course, the COVID pandemic virtually cancelled all corporate conventions and seminars last year. However, CSAV quickly proved adept at holding and producing virtual events. Companies like Generations Federal Credit Union held their quarterly shareholder meetings virtually, hosted by CSAV.
  • As our nation fully reopens and normalcy in our daily lives returns, CSAV is well-poised to provide audio-video services for all types of events ranging from charitable galas to corporate seminars and trade shows. CSAV has already proven to be a valuable asset to both PM Group clients and outside organizations that seek a financially efficient audio-video services provider. CSAV was built to be a low-cost, quality provider of convention services from its inception. Are you having an event and need audio-video services? Call Juan Juarez and let this 16-year veteran put together a winning event plan that will have you smiling!

 

 

__________________________________

 

 

In summary, The PM Group has built a vast array of in-house services that provides all agency clients all their marketing needs with one point of contact. Our proactive, full service approach to addressing client needs creates an invaluable list of resources to save our clients both time and money, while enhancing the client lifespan. This is what is expected from the San Antonio’s advertising agency leader: continuity, results, stability, award-winning work, full transparency and demonstrated value.

 

 

 

 

About The PM Group: The PM Group was founded in 1988. For over 33 years, the agency has continued to grow and is San Antonio’s largest marketing and advertising agency. The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including home services, automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profits, real estate and fitness. For two consecutive years now, the Agency has set annual revenue records with the addition of six new clients and through the continued success of our existing clients. Additionally, the agency hosts their annual Charity Gala that to date has raised millions of dollars for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! In 2020 alone, the Agency donated over $1,050,000 to local non-profits. It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
It was ‘Christmas in May’ as dozens of The PM Group and Noisy Trumpet staffers gathered to present a grand total of $226,792 to five amazing community non-profits that work tirelessly to make our community a better place.

 

 

Representatives from the Brighton Center, SA YES, the San Antonio Museum of Art and P.A.M Media Outreach all participated in the recent check presentations. The 100 Club and San Antonio Sports all received generous donations from the agency as well at separate presentations.

 

 

The PM Group has held multiple fundraising events during the first five months of 2021, while raising and donating over $311,000 total so far this year.

 

 

“Our companies have a legacy of community support and charitable giving through our ‘Volunteerism Matters’ employee program. I couldn’t be any prouder of our team for their dedicated commitment to making our community a better place,” stated Fran Yanity, President of both Noisy Trumpet and The PM Group and Board Member of numerous non-profit boards.

 

 

“Corporate support for 501c non-profits has been declining nationally for several years now. One of the few exceptions here in San Antonio would be the charitable giving from The PM Group and Noisy Trumpet,” stated SA YES Executive Director Danielle Gunter. “Their compassion for underprivileged school children has been on display for over 20 years now,” Gunter added.

 

 

Joining the PM Group and Noisy Trumpet team at the presentation were Tatiana Herrera-Schneider with San Antonio Museum of Art, Kim Jefferies and Katrina Campbell from Brighton Center, Paula Monroe with P.A.M. Media Outreach and Danielle Gunter with SA YES.

 

 

“As the San Antonio advertising and marketing agency leader, we have an obligation to help make our community a better place. We take great pride in our charitable giving and community support. I firmly believe that we owe much of our success to the kindness in our hearts that drives our giving culture,” said The PM Group’s CEO and Founder, Bob Wills.

 

 

In 2020, Wills spearheaded charitable fundraising at their companies and collectively raised over $1,076,000 for local non-profit groups benefitting organizations that serve and provide for military veterans, battered woman, childhood services, educational support and pet rescue. Just recently, The PM Group provided Christy’s Hope with a check for $40,500 at their annual fundraising gala to assist in their crusade to support battered woman and children. Giving to community non-profits will remain at the heart of what they do as an agency.

The PM Group’s client roster spans numerous industries including home services, automotive, CPG, entertainment and sports marketing, legal, business services, non-profits, real estate and fitness. For two consecutive years now, the Agency has set annual revenue records with the addition of six new clients and through the continued success of our existing clients. Additionally, the agency hosts their annual Charity Gala that to date has raised millions of dollars for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! In 2020 alone, the Agency donated over $1,050,000 to local non-profits. It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
This past Sunday, our latest publication of Making a Difference in San Antonio featured one of our nonprofit clients, Soldiers’ Angels. Our relationship with Soldiers’ Angels began in 2018 when we hosted them at our offices for a PM Group community service project. Our team made no-sew blankets for veterans in local VA hospitals as we learned about the great impact Soldier’s Angels has on our community locally, nationally, and across the world. The recent feature in the San Antonio Express-News explains this impact in even greater detail and educates our community on the numerous opportunities to get involved as well.

 

 

Soldiers’ Angels has several programs they conduct annually including their Deployed Support Program, Family Support Program, Wounded Support Program, and Veteran Support Program. Within each program, volunteers put in hundreds of hours baking for families and soldiers, writing letters of encouragement and support, sewing and crafting for families at home, and even collecting and distributing baby items for little ones and that’s only just a few of the ways these programs support our military. These are also just their programs, they also host annual campaigns throughout the year to collect and make the top items soldiers need. Some of these campaigns are their Valentines for Veterans, Warm Feet for Warriors, and Holiday Stockings for Heroes. With the help of volunteers and generous community donors, each campaign gives away thousands to tens of thousands of items to deployed soldiers.

 

 

To say Soldiers’ Angels makes a big impact on our active duty and Veteran military community is an understatement. Since its founding, they have provided more than $205 million in aid for military, veterans, and their families. In 2020 alone, they adapted to include efforts to support during the COVID-19 pandemic and helped over 40,300 Veteran families with food support and they distributed over 413,000 bottles of hand sanitizer to combat deployed, active duty, VA Staff, and volunteers at food distributions. We are honored to work with and feature an organization that works so hard to lift up such an important group of individuals in our country.

For two consecutive years now, the Agency has set annual revenue records with the addition of six new clients and through the continued success of our existing clients. Additionally, the agency hosts their annual Charity Gala that to date has raised millions of dollars for local San Antonio area non-profit organizations in the last 15 years! In 2020 alone, the Agency donated over $1,050,000 to local non-profits. It is our way of giving back to the community that we so humbly serve.

node.content:
The San Antonio Museum of Art  adds richness of art, inspiration and cultural experiences to our community through recent exhibitions like Movie Metal, No Ocean Between Us, and Texas Women: A New History of Abstract Art. But art museums like SAMA heavily rely on funding these exhibitions through fundraising, special events, charitable giving, museum visitors and memberships. After a tough year in attendance last year, SAMA’s 2021 annual membership push is more critical than ever – and we are up for this challenge.

 

 

Launching and increasing the number of total memberships for the museum was only one of our agency’s goals as the museum celebrates their 40th anniversary. We also wanted to raise the overall awareness of SAMA in the San Antonio community as well as drive traffic to their website and the museum itself. Their new campaign blitz included a variety of tactics as we worked in collaboration with public relations & digital firm Noisy Trumpet. With a direct mail piece, TV and radio commercials, social and digital ads, and morning show television appearances, we knew that their message would be heard and reinforced by using different multi-media channels to get the word out.

 

 

With a San Antonio Museum of Art membership, each member receives unlimited free general admission, unlimited free admission to special exhibitions, invitations to exhibition previews, a discount at the Museum Shop and Tre Trattoria restaurant, and so much more. Membership perks for joining are great, but SAMA is also rewarding any person that joins by July 31st, they’ll receive a one-time offer of special free gifts and one extra month of membership free!
